description: 1939–1945 global conflict
description_wiki: World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

description: public holiday commemorating the surrender of Nazi Germany (8 May 1945)
description_wiki: Victory in Europe Day is the day celebrating the formal acceptance by the Allies of World War II of Nazi Germany's unconditional surrender of its armed forces on Tuesday, 8 May 1945; it marked the official surrender of all German military operations.

description: 1941 Japanese surprise attack on the U.S
description_wiki: On December 7, 1941, the Empire of Japan launched a surprise military strike on the United States Pacific Fleet at its naval base at Pearl Harbor on Oahu, Hawaii.

description: reserve force of the United States Army and Air Force
description_wiki: The National Guard is a military reserve organization of the United States Department of Defense (DoD).

description: air warfare and space branch of the Canadian Armed Forces
description_wiki: The Royal Canadian Air Force (RCAF; French: Aviation royale canadienne - ARC, lit. 'Canadian royal aviation') is the air and space force of Canada.

surface_forms: United States Merchant Marine Academy at Kings Point
description: U.S. service academy
description_wiki: The United States Merchant Marine Academy (USMMA or Kings Point) is a United States service academy in Kings Point, New York.

description: city in San Mateo County, California, United States
description_wiki: San Mateo is the most populous city in San Mateo County, California, United States, on the San Francisco Peninsula.

description: American government agency
description_wiki: The United States Maritime Service (USMS) was established in 1938 under the provisions of the Merchant Marine Act of 1936 as voluntary training organization to train individuals to become officers and crew members on merchant ships that form the United States Merchant Marine per 46 U.S.C. § 51701.

description: Reserve Component (RC) of the United States Navy
description_wiki: The United States Navy Reserve (USNR), known as the United States Naval Reserve from 1915 to 2004, is the Reserve Component (RC) of the United States Navy.

description: country in Central Europe
description_wiki: Poland, officially the Republic of Poland, is a country in Central Europe.

aliases: The City So Nice They Named It Twice
description: most populous city in the United States
description_wiki: New York, often called New York City (NYC), is the most populous city in the United States.

description: major formation of the United States Army in the Western Front of World War I
description_wiki: The American Expeditionary Forces (AEF) was a formation of the United States Armed Forces on the Western Front during World War I, composed mostly of units from the U.S. Army.

description: sovereign state in West Africa
description_wiki: Liberia, officially the Republic of Liberia, is a country on the West African coast.

description: sovereign state in Central America
description_wiki: Panama, officially the Republic of Panama, is a country located at the southern end of Central America in North America, bordering South America.

description: government agency
description_wiki: The War Shipping Administration (WSA) was a World War II emergency war agency of the US government, tasked to purchase and operate the civilian shipping tonnage the United States needed for fighting the war.

description: France company
description_wiki: American President Lines, LLC, is an American container shipping company that is a subsidiary of French shipping company CMA CGM. It operates an all-container ship fleet, including eleven U.S. flagged container vessels.

description: former United States shipping line
description_wiki: Lykes Bros.

description: US federal agency (1936–1950)
description_wiki: The United States Maritime Commission was an independent executive agency of the U.S. federal government that was created by the Merchant Marine Act of 1936, which was passed by Congress on June 29, 1936, and was abolished on May 24, 1950.

surface_forms: United States West Point Army Academy
aliases: U.S. Military Academy at West Point
United States Military Academy at West Point
description: federal service academy in West Point, New York, United States
description_wiki: The United States Military Academy (USMA), commonly known as West Point, is a United States service academy in West Point, New York, that educates cadets for service as commissioned officers in the United States Army.

description: U.S. Navy federal service academy in the Annapolis, Maryland area
description_wiki: The United States Naval Academy (USNA, Navy, or Annapolis) is a federal service academy adjacent to Annapolis, Maryland.

surface_forms: Coast Guard Academy in New London, Connecticut
aliases: Revenue Cutter Service School of Instruction
description: U.S. federal service academy for the Coast Guard
description_wiki: The United States Coast Guard Academy (USCGA), located in New London, Connecticut, is the U.S. service academy specifically for the United States Coast Guard.

surface_forms: the Air Force Academy in Colorado
description: military academy near Colorado Springs, Colorado, USA
description_wiki: The United States Air Force Academy (USAFA) is a United States service academy in Air Force Academy, Colorado, immediately north of Colorado Springs.

description: northern area of the Atlantic ocean
liberty ship of WWII
description_wiki: SS Johns Hopkins was a Liberty ship built in the United States during World War II. She was named after Johns Hopkins, was an American merchant, investor, and philanthropist.

description: country in Central Europe
description_wiki: Germany, officially the Federal Republic of Germany, is a country in Western and Central Europe.

description: 1944 landing operations of the Allied invasion of Normandy
description_wiki: The Normandy landings were the landing operations and associated airborne operations on 6 June 1944 of the Allied invasion of Normandy in Operation Overlord during the Second World War.

description: consolidated city and county in California, United States
description_wiki: San Francisco, officially the City and County of San Francisco, is the fourth-most populous city in California and the 17th-most populous in the United States, with a population of 826,079 in 2025.

description: city and county seat of Alameda County, California, United States
description_wiki: Oakland is a city in the East Bay region of the San Francisco Bay Area in the U.S. state of California.

description: 1945 major battle of the Pacific War
description_wiki: The Battle of Okinawa (Japanese: 沖縄戦, Hepburn: Okinawa-sen), codenamed Operation Iceberg, was a major battle of the Pacific War fought on the island of Okinawa by the United States Army and United States Marine Corps forces against the Imperial Japanese Army.

description: neighborhood city of Los Angeles, California, United States
city in Los Angeles County, California, United States
description_wiki: Long Beach is a coastal city in southeastern Los Angeles County, California, United States.

description: large canal in Panama
description_wiki: The Panama Canal (Spanish: Canal de Panamá) is an artificial 82-kilometer (51-mile) waterway in Panama that connects the Caribbean Sea with the Pacific Ocean.

description: city in Maryland, United States
description_wiki: Baltimore ( BAWL-tim-or, locally:  BAWL-dim-OR or  BAWL-mər), also known as Baltimore City, is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Maryland.

aliases: War to Resist US Aggression and Aid Korea
description: war between North and South Korea, 1950–1953
description_wiki: The Korean War (25 June 1950 – 27 July 1953) was an armed conflict the Korean Peninsula fought between North Korea (Democratic People's Republic of Korea; DPRK) and South Korea (Republic of Korea; ROK) and their allies.

description: United States Navy admiral, Chief of Naval Operations (1878-1956)
description_wiki: Ernest Joseph King (23 November 1878 – 25 June 1956) was a fleet admiral in the United States Navy who served as Commander in Chief, United States Fleet (COMINCH) and Chief of Naval Operations (CNO) during World War II. Franklin Delano Roosevelt appointed King to command global American strategy during World War II and he held supreme naval command in his unprecedented double capacity as COMINCH and CNO. He was the U.S. Navy's second-most senior officer in World War II after Fleet Admiral William D. Leahy, who served as Chief of Staff to the Commander in Chief.

description: class of cargo ship
description_wiki: The Victory ship is a class of cargo ships produced in large numbers by American shipyards during World War II. They were a more modern design compared to the earlier Liberty ship, were slightly larger and had more powerful steam turbine engines, giving them higher speed to allow participation in high-speed convoys and make them more difficult targets for German U-boats.

description: cargo ships built in the United States during World War II
description_wiki: Liberty ships are a class of cargo ship built in the United States during World War II under the Emergency Shipbuilding Program.
